description: Remove dead response.ok checks after fetchFromPage/doFetch in github-api.ts and get-user-profile.ts, and replace a plain Error throw in create-label.ts with ToolError.internal.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/github && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Remove unreachable response.ok checks in github-api.ts and get-user-profile.ts
userStories.description: In plugins/github/src/github-api.ts, three functions (pageJson line 111, graphql line 261, formPost line 348) check `if (!response.ok) return classifyError(...)` after doFetch(), which wraps fetchFromPage(). Since fetchFromPage throws on non-ok responses, these checks are unreachable dead code — classifyError() can never be called from these branches. The same dead pattern exists in plugins/github/src/tools/get-user-profile.ts at line 29. Remove all four dead checks.
acceptanceCriteria: The `if (!response.ok) return classifyError(...)` line is removed from pageJson() in github-api.ts
The `if (!response.ok) return classifyError(...)` line is removed from graphql() in github-api.ts
The `if (!response.ok) return classifyError(...)` line is removed from formPost() in github-api.ts
The `if (!response.ok)` block is removed from get-user-profile.ts
classifyError() is still defined and used elsewhere (it remains in the file, just not called from these dead branches)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/github/src/github-api.ts (remove dead if (!response.ok) at lines 111, 261, 348)
- plugins/github/src/tools/get-user-profile.ts (remove dead if (!response.ok) at line ~29)

Context:
Each of these functions calls `const response = await doFetch(url, init)`. doFetch() (lines 77-86) calls fetchFromPage() which auto-throws on non-ok responses. doFetch() re-throws ToolError and wraps other errors, so it NEVER returns a non-ok Response.

Deadcode in pageJson() (line 111):
  if (!response.ok) return classifyError(response, path); // UNREACHABLE

Deadcode in graphql() (line 261):
  if (!response.ok) return classifyError(response, '/_graphql'); // UNREACHABLE

Deadcode in formPost() (line 348):
  if (!response.ok) return classifyError(response, postPath); // UNREACHABLE

Deadcode in get-user-profile.ts (line ~29):
  if (!response.ok) { ... } // UNREACHABLE

Simply delete these lines. The code after each dead check is the actual processing (response.json(), etc.) which becomes the direct continuation.

Constraints:
- Do NOT delete the classifyError() function definition — it is used by doFetch() which wraps fetchFromPage errors
- Do NOT change the response.json() or response.text() calls that follow the dead checks
- In formPost(), the code after the dead check handles successful responses — keep all of that

userStories.title: Replace throw new Error() in create-label.ts with ToolError.internal()
userStories.description: In plugins/github/src/tools/create-label.ts line 30, `throw new Error('Could not determine repository ID')` throws a plain Error that bypasses the ToolError dispatch chain. Replace with ToolError.internal().
acceptanceCriteria: Line 30 in create-label.ts uses ToolError.internal('Could not determine repository ID') instead of new Error()
ToolError is imported from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk' in create-label.ts
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/github/src/tools/create-label.ts (line 30)

Context:
Current line 30:
  if (!repoId) throw new Error('Could not determine repository ID');

Change to:
  if (!repoId) throw ToolError.internal('Could not determine repository ID');

Add ToolError to the import from '@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk' if not already present.
